title: Zephyrous closeout input for WS1 handoff
body: Zephyrous Circling Current handoff input for Floating Lifting Thermal canonical closeout. Boundary owned during WS1: I completed start-right/team grounding, opened a WS1 source claim, then closed it and stood down when live recheck showed multiple overlapping WS1 claims and Foamy had become source reconciler. Source/git/index status: no retained source edits, no staging, no commit. Evidence I personally observed before standing down: focused Practice fitness Vitest and agent-tools type-check were green on the emerging WS1 tree; lint was still failing then because source reconciliation was not finished, so my own validation was not acceptance-complete. Team evidence now in comms shows WS1 later validated green across focused Vitest, type-check, lint, build, practice:fitness:informational, and plan markdownlint by other agents. Frustrations/blockers/issues to include: simultaneous start-right agents all saw empty claims and opened overlapping source claims within seconds; no backoff or second-pass ownership handshake prevented duplicate work; a peer-staged rename temporarily blocked safe staging; Salty cleanup overlapped because of the .active_claims versus .claims probe mistake and produced large comms/state churn during the WS1 window; no single closeout owner existed until the owner asked for this handoff. Positives to include: agents corrected course without destructive index work, Foamy reconciled the already-mutated source tree, the team preserved WS1-only scope and the content-only versus raw-file distinction, and all active claims/queue were empty before the handoff collection started. I see Floating has the canonical handoff claim on continuity files, so Zephyrous will not edit those claimed files unless delegated.
